status: RE-RUN 2026-07-09 on the m7a.4xlarge box. Explore + BI on Fluree v4.1.2 (13a78d2a); Update on Fluree v4.1.2 (e563e9bb = 13a78d2a + the single-pattern DELETE-WHERE stats-view fix). Real BSBM: bsbmtools-0.2 test driver (randomized query mix -> QMpH), x {1,4,8,16,32} clients, 0 timeouts. vs the 25d8c28f baseline: Explore +8-14% at low/mid concurrency (flat at saturation), BI +38-43%, and Update +0.8 to +4.6% (the e563e9bb fix removed the -3 to -7% concurrent-write regression that 13a78d2a alone had). All three use cases now beat the prior baseline.

dataset.name: BSBM 1M (pc=2785, forward-chained)
dataset.description: Berlin SPARQL Benchmark e-commerce dataset, canonical 1M scale point. Deterministic generator (no seed); pin = toolkit SHA + -pc + flags.
dataset.entities: 4,745 product features; 60 producers / 2,785 products (5 types); 29 vendors / 55,700 offers; 4 rating sites / 1,417 reviewers / 27,850 reviews

hardware.notes: Same instance family/size as the dblp-core run for CPU/clock comparability; disk bumped 250->400 GB for multi-scale data.

methodology.notes: BSBM randomizes query params per mix iteration, neutralizing result caches. Server stays up; explore/bi share one ledger. Update runs on a FRESH pristine ledger per client-count. reindex_min_bytes=2MB. Server = raw fluree-server (--indexing-enabled).

fluree.label: Fluree
fluree.commit: Explore + BI: 13a78d2a8cb5a0582830d823ac424e13c4e06bb0. Update: e563e9bb334402b2e21d8637747e15ef47b55520 (= 13a78d2a + 'skip per-update stats-view rebuild for single-pattern WHERE'; fixes the write regression, reads unchanged). Both 2026-07-09.
fluree.install: Built from source (cargo build --release -p fluree-db-cli -p fluree-db-server). Binary reports 'fluree 4.1.1' (pre-release build of v4.1.2).
fluree.config: default server config; LeafletCache budget ~21.5 GiB

caveats: Real BSBM via bsbmtools-0.2 (randomized query mix, QMpH), not a SPARQLoscope-style per-query latency reshape.

pc=2785 is the canonical '1M' scale point; measured triple count is 724,101 (depends on toolkit version + forward chaining). Absolute QMpH is comparable engine-vs-engine on this box, not across toolkit versions/scales.
1M is the harness-validation scale; headline numbers come from 100M (pc=284826) and 200M (pc=570000).

MIXED-COMMIT REPORT: Explore + BI are on 13a78d2a; Update is on e563e9bb (= 13a78d2a + the DELETE-WHERE stats-view fix). 13a78d2a alone regressed the concurrent write path -3 to -7% (root cause: execute_where_streaming rebuilt the novelty-merged stats view on every single-pattern update WHERE); e563e9bb skips that rebuild where there is no join order to choose, restoring Update to +0.8 to +4.6% over the 25d8c28f baseline with reads unchanged. The 100M/200M Explore+BI grids remain 13a78d2a (this fix is write-only; reads are identical). runs/{explore,bi}__*.xml are the 13a78d2a run; runs/update__*.xml are the e563e9bb run.
